assertions.details: Secret migration works correctly for Telegram bot token.

assertions.evidence: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not found in .env when running without --migrate-secrets. Report shows: channels-secrets skipped - 'Secrets not migrated (use --migrate-secrets): TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN'.
assertions.details: Secrets correctly excluded when flag not set.

assertions.evidence: WHATSAPP_ENABLED=1 found in .env when source has whatsapp.enabled=true.
assertions.details: Non-secret channel setting migrated without needing --migrate-secrets.

assertions.evidence: When .env pre-existing value TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=original-token-12345 exists and migration runs with --migrate-secrets (no --overwrite), the original line is preserved BUT a duplicate TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123456789:... is appended. Result: .env has TWO TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN lines.
assertions.details: Root cause: In updateEnvFile(), when !this.args.overwrite, existing lines are kept (line 1372: newLines.push(line)) but the key is NOT added to updatedKeys (only added when this.args.overwrite is true). The 'Add new keys' loop (line 1385) then appends the key again because !updatedKeys.has(key). Fix: Add updatedKeys.add(key) in the else branch at line 1372.

assertions.evidence: Both TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=tg-token-123 and WHATSAPP_ENABLED=1 found in .env after migration from source with both telegram and whatsapp configured.
assertions.details: Multiple channels migrated in single run.

blockers.description: updateEnvFile() has a bug: when --overwrite is not set, existing .env keys are preserved but NOT tracked in updatedKeys, causing duplicate key lines to be appended. Same bug affects VAL-MODEL-006.
blockers.suggestedFix: In scripts/migrate-to-nanocore.ts updateEnvFile(), around line 1372, add updatedKeys.add(key) in the else branch (when not overwriting) so the key is not appended again.
